commit.message:
Refactor CLI to interactive flow with figprd command and speed optimizations

- Interactive prompt for Figma URL and token (no CLI flags required)
- Add figprd entry point via pyproject.toml
- Auto-detect fastest available Ollama model
- Parallel Figma frame fetching with ThreadPoolExecutor
- Reduce API calls: node URL = 1 call, full file = 2 small calls
- Skip comments fetch, add 30s timeout and 429 retry logic
- Shorten LLM prompts and reduce token limits for faster generation
- Fix pydantic version to >=2.12.0 for Python 3.14 compatibility

commit.message:
Add batch bootstrapping support (numbatch=2)

Implement batch processing for IKS and BlindRotate to process multiple
TLWE ciphertexts in a single bootstrapping pass. Key changes:
- IKS: batch TLWE buffering with SyncReadMem, LoadState FSM, acceptInput gating
- BR: batch TRLWE in BRmem, batch loop in RUN state, amem pre-read to
  compensate SyncReadMem 1-cycle latency for correct PMBX addressing
- Test: batch-aware verification for PMBX, feedback, and output
- Standalone test build (testbuild/CMakeLists.txt)

Note: This reverts IKS sub-accumulator optimization and uint16_t lvl0param
changes (qbit=32). These can be re-applied on top of batch support.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Use www.azanw.com as canonical domain in all meta tags and URLs

azanw.com 307-redirects to www.azanw.com, so OG image URLs pointing
to azanw.com forced crawlers to follow a redirect. Some crawlers
(especially WhatsApp) may skip images behind redirects. Updated all
og:image, og:url, twitter:image, and sitemap URLs to use the
canonical www.azanw.com domain directly.

feat: gravity well orbits based on composite health score

Health score (0=perfect, 1=critical) combines:
- Latency vs threshold proximity (40%)
- Uptime deficit (30%)
- Packet loss from sparkline (30%)

Orbit radius maps directly to health score:
healthy hosts orbit close to core, degraded hosts drift outward.

commit.message:
fix: bid formula - starts at 10% of prize, +0.05 every 10 clicks, max 50%

- Starting bid = prizeValue * 10% (e.g., 10 USDT for 100 USDT prize)
- Each click adds prizeValue * discount_per_click (0.005 USDT)
- Display floors to nearest 0.05 (jumps every ~10 clicks)
- Bid capped at 50% of prize value (50 USDT max)
- DB: max_discount_pct=0.40, discount_per_click=0.00005

* feat(gemini): add sub-agent generation to converter

Add generate_gemini_agents() function that converts Claude Code agents
(arckit-claude/agents/) into Gemini CLI sub-agent format. The conversion:
- Keeps name and description from frontmatter, drops model
- Adds max_turns: 25 and timeout_mins: 10
- Rewrites ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} paths to ~/.gemini/extensions/arckit
- Rewrites Read instructions to shell cat commands
- Prepends EXTENSION_FILE_ACCESS_BLOCK to prompt body

Generates 6 sub-agent files in arckit-gemini/agents/.

Port all 6 Claude Code hooks to Python for Gemini CLI:
- hook_utils.py: shared utilities (DOC_TYPES, parsing, repo discovery)
- session-start.py: SessionStart hook with version and project detection
- context-inject.py: BeforeAgent hook with project inventory injection
- validate-filename.py: BeforeTool hook for ARC filename normalization
- file-protection.py: BeforeTool hook blocking sensitive file edits
- update-manifest.py: AfterTool hook for manifest.json auto-update

commit.message:
Add cbdb-local skill for offline CBDB queries via SQLite

Local querying of 656K+ historical Chinese figures. Downloads CBDB SQLite
database (~69MB compressed, ~556MB uncompressed), creates search indexes,
and provides CLI/Python API for person search, kinship, offices, social
associations, addresses, exam entries, and raw SQL. Cross-platform 7z
extraction (macOS/Windows/Linux).

fix: handle implicit flow recovery token in login page

When Supabase uses implicit flow, the access_token arrives as a URL
hash fragment on /login. Detect type=recovery and redirect to
/reset-password so the user can set a new password.

commit.message:
Fix comment tier badges to use universal all-time points system

The highlights on restaurant pages were using a separate category-count-based
tier system scoped to the comment's year, causing tier badges and auras to
not match the user's actual current tier. Now uses the same all-time
points-based getUserTier() from user-points.ts that the critic profile and
navbar use, so tiers are universal and never regress across years.

Record lessons: defensive validation, Stop and Error node, Filter silent drops

- Filter nodes silently drop non-matching items (onError only fires
  on expression errors, not condition failures)
- Use IF + Stop and Error for data shape validation that should halt
  the workflow on invalid items
- Stop and Error node is the purpose-built tool for halting with a
  custom error message (not Code node with throw)

commit.message:
feat: add per-question answer detail view on results page

Clicking an index number in the results table opens a modal showing
a full per-question breakdown:
- Type 1: marked option highlighted green (correct) or red (wrong),
  correct answer shown, tick/cross result indicator
- Type 2: per-sub-option T/F grid with colour-coded correct/wrong cells
  and the expected answer shown beneath, plus partial score

Backend: new GET /exams/{id}/results/{index_number}/detail endpoint joins
Result + Submission raw_answers + Questions + AnswerKeys into a single
QuestionDetail list. Added QuestionDetail and ResultDetail Pydantic schemas.

commit.message:
fix: handle strerror_r() return type at compile time via overloading

The cmake check_cxx_source_compiles for strerror_r return type is
unreliable on glibc 2.42+ with -std=c++17: the isolated cmake check
sees the XSI variant (returns int) because __STRICT_ANSI__ suppresses
_GNU_SOURCE, but during actual compilation other headers in the include
chain define _GNU_SOURCE, switching strerror_r to the GNU variant
(returns char*). This caused a compile error on the test-perf CI job
(Ubuntu 26.04, GCC 15.2.0).

Replace the cmake-time STRERROR_R_CHAR_P detection with C++ function
overloading that resolves the correct handler at compile time regardless
of which strerror_r variant is active.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: min topN=5 in bear market, add cash reserve mechanism

- backtest.py: add _precompute_market_200ma_bear(); after seasonal filter
  apply topN floor (min 5 normal / min 3 extreme bear weekly_drop<-10%),
  RSI>70 filter in bear env, atr_inv z-score +0.3x boost, 30% cash reserve
  when market below 200-day MA
- daily_pick.py: initialize weekly_drop=None before market filter block;
  load max(ma_days,200) days for 200MA check; add market_below_200ma
  detection; topN floor after seasonal filter; bear mode RSI filter and
  atr_inv score boost in model scoring section

Verified: 93 tests pass; key incident 2025-03-31 topN=1→5 (-20.08%→-5.80%);
max weekly loss improved from -20.08% to -9.03% (target <-10% achieved)

commit.message:
Make rejection filter evidence-aware for thin data scenarios

When a candidate has < 5 evidence pieces, Rules 1-3 downgrade from
hard rejection to risk flags. This prevents blanket rejection when
only a few collectors return data (e.g., no proxy credits).
R4 (easeToBuild) still hard-rejects regardless of evidence depth.

- Add try/catch around resp.json() for non-JSON error responses (e.g. Vercel HTML error pages)
- Add null checks for thinkingEl in all error paths to prevent TypeError

commit.message:
Critical fix: propagate AI sync to cloud + preserve offline card photos

- Use updateProduct/updateSupplier from db.js instead of db.*.update()
  so changes trigger cloud sync push to other devices
- Process suppliers FIRST so products can link to them
- When card captured offline with no name extracted, create supplier with
  placeholder name to preserve the card photo for later AI processing
- Re-process supplier cards with placeholder names when back online
- Update linked products' supplierCompany after card is processed
- Always dispatch refresh event after any sync changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix reconciliation gap: revert graded bets with no backend confirmation

Phase 3 reconciliation previously skipped bets with no backend match
(`if (!backendMatch) continue`). This left incorrectly graded bets
(like Alex Tuch POINTS from the `or True` bug) stuck as W/L forever.

Now: if a graded prop bet has no matching entry in the results files AND
the bet is recent (≤48h), revert it to pending. Old bets are untouched.
